truthAudit.Summary: The audited Run 2 output correctly identified four of the ten true vulnerabilities, but it misclassified their CWE identifiers and severity levels for three of them. No false positives were reported.

Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the uncontrolled format string vulnerability in the logging function, which allows an attacker to supply a malicious format string via user input. The evidence snippet shows the direct use of `printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input);` with a user-controlled format string.

The previous answer correctly flagged the command injection vulnerability in the configuration validation routine, where the command string is constructed from configuration values and executed via `system()`. The evidence shows the direct use of `system(command.c_str());` with user-supplied inputs.

The previous answer correctly identified the buffer overflow caused by the unchecked `strcpy` into `input_buffer_` when the input length exceeds the buffer size. The evidence demonstrates the unsafe copy operation.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "0374:             for (const auto& token : tokens) expr_str += token.text + " ";\n0375:             if (expr_str.length() > 0) strcpy(expression_buffer_, expr_str.c_str());"
Items.Notes: The previous answer correctly identified the heap overflow in the expression parser caused by an unchecked `strcpy` into the 256‑byte `expression_buffer_`. The evidence shows the unsafe copy operation.

Items.Key: unbounded strcpy leading to buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-119|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|83|84|evaluate_expression|0831: if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { 0832: strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); 0833: } else { 0834: string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_));|overflow can corrupt memory, leading to arbitrary code execution or program crash.|when user input length is greater than or equal to stack_buffer_size (1024).|use a safe copy routine that checks the length, e.g., safe_string_copy with dest_size-1, or replace strcpy with strncpy and ensure null termination.->unbounded strcpy leading to buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-119|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|83|84|evaluate_expression|0831: if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { 0832: strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); 0833: } else { 0834: string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_));|overflow can corrupt memory, leading to arbitrary code execution or program crash.|when user input length is greater than or equal to stack_buffer_size (1024).|use a safe copy routine that checks the length, e.g., safe_string_copy with dest_size-1, or replace strcpy with strncpy and ensure null termination.

command injection via system() in execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|0605: if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { 0606: std::string cmd = command.substr(5); 0607: std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; 0608: system(cmd.c_str()); 0609: }|an attacker can execute arbitrary shell commands via the admin interface.|when a user enters an admin command starting with "exec".|avoid using system(). instead, validate the command against a whitelist or use a safer api such as execve with explicit arguments.->command injection via system() in execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|605|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|0605: if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { 0606: std::string cmd = command.substr(5); 0607: std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; 0608: system(cmd.c_str()); 0609: }|an attacker can execute arbitrary shell commands via the admin interface.|when a user enters an admin command starting with "exec".|avoid using system(). instead, validate the command against a whitelist or use a safer api such as execve with explicit arguments.
